retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Describe the anatomy of the rectum in detail. Identify the indications for rectal microsurgery. Outline the equipment, personnel, preparation, and different platforms in rectal microsurgery. Explain the potential complications in rectal microsurgery, Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Anal Fistulotomy(Archived) -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Fistula in ano is a sequela of a perianal abscess, which appears to be a simple diagnosis and requires surgical treatment. There is no role for antibiotics for a fistula if the abscess is drained. Surgical exploration is necessary to delineate the fistula tract and to drain any undrained abscesses associated with the formation of the tract. Treatment can be either with fistulotomy or Seton placement (cutting vs. non-cutting) and is best assessed by an experienced surgeon or colorectal surgeon. They are best served with an interprofessional approach involving gastrointestinal physicians experienced in Crohn disease, gynecologists, plastic surgeons, surgical oncologists, and colorectal surgeons who can evaluate the entire perineum of the patient and ensure that systemic therapy or oncologic therapies are employed.

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Preparation -- Pre-operative Work-up
retrieved_docs.content: The standard of care for rectal microsurgery can be variable across different centers due to a lack of uniformity and standardization. The work-up and decision-making process are the same for TEMS and TAMIS. Once a rectal lesion has been identified and considered suitable for localized surgery, a full colonoscopy should be performed to exclude any synchronous tumors elsewhere in the colon. The rectal lesion must be accurately localized, with its position and orientation carefully documented.

retrieved_docs.title: Crohn Disease -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Colorectal surgeons may be involved if patients present with complications like fistulae and intestinal obstruction.

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: An interprofessional approach to colorectal cancer is crucial for achieving good patient outcomes. Rectal microsurgery is an emerging technique. Knowledge of patient selection and procedural steps are essential for good postoperative care and early identification of potential complications utilizing clinicians (MDs, DOs, PAs, and NPs), nursing staff, and in some cases, pharmacists. Teamwork and open communication are necessary to establish effective communication and advancement of clinical knowledge aimed at optimizing care for patients and reducing unnecessary hospital stays.

retrieved_docs.title: Hemicolectomy -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: The interprofessional team approach in the workup for colorectal cancer has been shown to improve patient outcomes. [27] A discussion and consensus opinion on the best management strategy is considered vital to modern practice and facilitates communication between all specialties involved in patient care simultaneously.

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Personnel
retrieved_docs.content: The essential theatre team is composed of the surgeon, surgical assistant, anesthesiologist, and scrub nurse. Other members may also present to help position the patient, assist in the anesthetic process and retrieve equipment intraoperatively.

retrieved_docs.title: Internal Anal Sphincterotomy -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Anal fissures often lead to a poor quality of life because of inappropriate or inadequate treatment. The primary caregiver should always refer these patients to a colorectal surgeon because of the enormous morbidity. The condition is best managed by an interprofessional team that includes a colorectal surgeon, dietitian, general surgeon, and a gastroenterologist. A proper anal internal sphincterotomy is required in adults to prevent fecal incontinence.

retrieved_docs.title: Rectal Cancer Microsurgery -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Colorectal malignancies are considered the third most common cancer for both male and female individuals. Moreover, they are the second most common cause of malignancy-related mortalities in the United States. Rectal microsurgery is an emerging technique that offers an oncologic cure without compromising bowel function. As the repertoire of rectal surgery expands, a good overall understanding of new procedures and platforms is needed to ensure optimal patient selection. This activity reviews the indications, techniques, platforms, and complications of rectal microsurgery and the role of the interprofessional team in the workup and postoperative care of these patients.

retrieved_docs.title: Adenocarcinoma(Archived) -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Revision of guidelines is critical to incorporate better, practical, and more feasible methods. In situations where enough data isn't available, expert opinion from a specialist may be taken to help better guide management. All this is possible with effective communication and coordination of care amongst different healthcare providers. [54]

retrieved_docs.title: Anorectal Fistula -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Patient-centered care involves the patient in preoperative discussion in proposed decision making. Educating patients preoperatively may aid in decision making to manage post-operative expectations. General Surgeons see and manage the majority of patients with anal fistula; however, involving a colorectal surgeon in complex cases is essential as they are trained more specifically in these conditions than the general surgeon. For example, ACGME requirements for graduation of general surgery residents only require a total of 20 anorectal procedures, however, accredited colorectal surgery fellowships require a total of 60 anorectal procedures including 20 fistula procedures.

retrieved_docs.title: Hemicolectomy -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: As discussed above, preoperative assessment is crucial to the identification of patients unsuitable for anesthesia and those who are considered high-risk patients so that a thorough postoperative plan can be put in place preoperatively. The identification of those patients with cardiorespiratory issues requiring high care or intensive care beds is a prime example.

retrieved_docs.title: Small Bowel Resection -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify common indications for small bowel resection and how the surgical approach is modified based on the pathology. Review techniques for small bowel resection and anastomoses. Review potential complications after a small bowel resection. Outline the special post procedure care for patients in order to improve collaboration and coordination among the interprofessional team. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Mohs Micrographic Surgery Interpretation of Technical Problems Impacting Slide Quality -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Key stages such as debulking, tissue orientation, inking, and embedding directly impact slide quality and depend on both surgeon technique and histology technician expertise. Various slide mounting and cutting methods also play roles in preserving tissue structure, while freezing and staining steps ensure the tissue retains key histological features. Each technical process has specific challenges. Consistent methods tailored to individual tissue types (eg, cartilage or adipose) can significantly improve slide clarity, aid in accurate diagnosis and maintain MMS's high success rates. Additionally, while MMS is not a new technique for skin cancer treatment, clinicians in other fields or general dermatology may be unaware of the process. Therefore, an improved understanding of slide preparation helps referring clinicians counsel patients on the MMS process. This activity for healthcare professionals is designed to enhance the learner's competence in preparing MMS, recognizing common technical problems, and implementing an appropriate interprofessional management approach to improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: Abdominoperineal Resection -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify the key indications for abdominoperineal resection and recognize patients who may benefit from this procedure. Differentiate between various surgical techniques and approaches used in abdominoperineal resection, understanding their implications for patient outcomes. Select the most appropriate preoperative imaging and staging methods to plan for abdominoperineal resection effectively. Collaberate comprehensive care plans with an interprofessional healthcare team encompassing surgical, medical, and psychological aspects for patients undergoing abdominoperineal resection. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 4 -- Community 2754
retrieved_docs.content: Colonoscopy and biopsy is identified as a specific type of Surgical Procedures on the Colon and Rectum, indicating that it is a surgical intervention performed on the colon and rectum. At the same time, colonoscopy and biopsy is placed under the broader category of إجراءات تصوير البطن والجهاز الهضمي (abdominal and gastrointestinal imaging procedures), showing that it also belongs to the general group of imaging techniques used to evaluate the gastrointestinal tract. This dual classification underscores that colonoscopy and biopsy serves both as a targeted surgical procedure for the colon and rectum and as an integral component of comprehensive abdominal and gastrointestinal imaging in patient assessment and care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2870
retrieved_docs.content: Colonoscopy and biopsy is classified as a component of إجراءات تصوير البطن والجهاز الهضمي (abdominal and gastrointestinal imaging procedures) and also as part of التنظير الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي (gastrointestinal endoscopic procedures). This dual categorization reflects that colonoscopy with biopsy involves both imaging of the abdominal and digestive tract and the use of endoscopic techniques to directly visualize the colon and obtain tissue samples for diagnostic evaluation. Recognizing colonoscopy and biopsy within these broader procedural groups helps clinicians understand its role in comprehensive gastrointestinal assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 3 -- Community 2022
retrieved_docs.content: Exploratory laparotomy and ثقب معوي (intestinal perforation) are frequently observed together in clinical practice. An exploratory laparotomy is often performed when a ثقب معوي is suspected or identified, as the surgical approach allows direct visualization and management of the perforated bowel. Recognizing the co‑occurrence of these conditions is important for timely diagnosis and appropriate operative intervention, which can reduce morbidity and improve patient outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 4 -- Community 2733
retrieved_docs.content: Colonoscopy and biopsy is identified as the principal diagnostic procedure for colorectal cancer, corresponding to the PhenX data element that captures the detection time point for a patient in a nominal format. This procedure is classified as a specific type of surgical intervention on the colon and rectum, falling under the broader category of Surgical Procedures on the Colon and Rectum. By serving both as a key diagnostic tool for identifying colorectal malignancies and as a recognized surgical technique, colonoscopy and biopsy plays a central role in patient evaluation and management within colorectal disease care.
